decks.notes: The key concept of this deck is to quickly gather resources and protect your combo pieces while disrupting your opponent's game plan. By utilizing powerful mana acceleration cards such as Moxen and Black Lotus, along with countermagic like Force of Will and Mindbreak Trap, you can ensure that you have the necessary tools to execute your combo. Additionally, cards like Dig Through Time and Merchant Scroll help you find crucial pieces of your combo, while cards like Hullbreacher and Urza's Saga provide additional utility and value. Overall, this deck aims to assemble its combo and win the game efficiently.

The deck focuses on assembling the Coveted Jewel as quickly as possible, using various artifact tutors and accelerants like Tinker and Transmute Artifact. The goal is to generate a large amount of mana with cards like Grim Monolith and Mana Crypt, and then take advantage of the Jewel's ability to draw extra cards and create a huge advantage. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Force of Will and Sink into Stupor to protect the combo and disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The deck focuses on using Oath of Druids to quickly get powerful creatures like Atraxa, Grand Unifier onto the battlefield. It also includes control elements like Force of Will and Show and Tell to disrupt the opponent's strategy and protect its own game plan. The deck aims to use its powerful creatures and spells to control the game and outvalue the opponent, while also having the ability to quickly end the game with a strong board presence.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy through counterspells and removal spells while also utilizing efficient card draw and mana acceleration. It aims to control the board and outvalue the opponent by consistently having answers to threats and ways to generate card advantage. The inclusion of the companion Lurrus of the Dream-Den allows for additional value and flexibility in the deck's game plan. With a strong control package and powerful spells like Time Walk and Ancestral Recall, the deck aims to outmaneuver opponents and maintain control of the game.

This aggressive Vintage Bazaar deck focuses on quickly filling up the graveyard with cards like Bazaar of Baghdad and Serum Powder to cheat out powerful threats like Vengevine and Hollow One. It utilizes free counterspells like Force of Will and Force of Negation to protect its game plan while also disrupting the opponent with cards like Mindbreak Trap. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with its speed and resilience, using cards like Squee, Goblin Nabob and Master of Death to maintain card advantage and keep the pressure on.

The deck revolves around the combo of using Doomsday to set up a win condition by manipulating the order of cards in the deck. It includes powerful cards like Ancestral Recall, Black Lotus, and Force of Will to help set up the combo and protect it once it's in motion. The deck also includes cantrips like Brainstorm and Ponder to dig for the necessary pieces as well as disruption like Mental Misstep and Flusterstorm. The win condition typically involves using Thassa's Oracle to win the game on the spot after resolving Doomsday.

This deck aims to quickly assemble the Doomsday combo, which uses the card Doomsday to stack the top 5 cards of the library in a specific order to win the game. The deck is designed to draw cards, manipulate the library, and protect the combo with interaction and countermagic. The win condition is typically using Thassa's Oracle or alternate win conditions like The Fantasticar or Undercity Sewers. The deck also includes powerful cards like Ancestral Recall, Time Walk, and the Moxen to accelerate and control the game.

This control deck focuses on leveraging powerful spells and artifacts to control the game and generate card advantage. It utilizes cards like Force of Will, Mindbreak Trap, and Gifts Ungiven to counter opponent's threats and find key combo pieces. The inclusion of powerful mana sources like Tolarian Academy and Moxen accelerates the deck's strategy, while cards like Underworld Breach and Brain Freeze provide a win condition. Lurrus of the Dream-Den provides additional value by allowing players to recast key spells from the graveyard. Ultimately, the deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver opponents to secure victory.

The deck aims to quickly assemble a powerful board state using artifact mana acceleration, card draw, and Paradoxical Outcome to generate advantage. It leverages cards like The Fantasticar, Grim Monolith, and Mishra's Workshop to facilitate explosive plays, while also having disruption in the form of Force of Will and Sink into Stupor to protect the combo. Karn, the Great Creator provides additional utility, and Tinker can be used to fetch key artifacts like The One Ring. The deck combines speed, resilience, and versatility to overwhelm opponents in the early game.

The deck focuses on using artifact creatures and disruptive effects to control the game while rapidly deploying threats to overwhelm the opponent. It utilizes powerful artifact lands like Mishra's Workshop and Tolarian Academy to ramp up quickly and cast expensive spells. The main win condition is to disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Null Rod and Sphere of Resistance while beating them down with creatures like Golos, Tireless Pilgrim and Patchwork Automaton. With the added utility of cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Crucible of Worlds, the deck aims to lock down the opponent and seize victory.

This Vintage deck focuses on using artifact creatures and disruptive spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive plays. The deck utilizes Mishra's Workshop and other mana-producing artifacts to power out threats like Golos, Tireless Pilgrim, Scrawling Crawler, Patchwork Automaton, and Stonecoil Serpent. Crucible of Worlds and Wasteland provide land destruction, while Null Rod and Sphere of Resistance disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck aims to lock down the board with various taxing effects and generate card advantage through Mystic Forge and Golos' ability to search for additional threats.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo pieces needed to cast Doomsday and win the game with Thassa's Oracle. The deck utilizes cards like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal to generate mana quickly, while cards like Preordain and Ponder help to dig for the necessary combo pieces. The Fantasticar can be used to protect key cards from removal or disruption, while Force of Will and Force of Negation provide additional protection. Overall, the deck aims to efficiently combo off and win the game before the opponent has a chance to respond.

This deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of powerful artifacts and creatures, utilizing cards such as Mishra's Workshop, Grim Monolith, and Ancient Tomb to quickly ramp up mana. The strategy revolves around generating significant resources early on to play threats like Glaring Fleshraker and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain, while also utilizing artifacts like Time Vault and The One Ring to gain additional advantage. The inclusion of cards like Chalice of the Void and Pithing Needle helps to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while cards like Mystic Forge and Sensei's Divining Top provide card selection and advantage.

This Vintage 1 vs 1 Aggro-MUD deck aims to overwhelm opponents with powerful artifacts and aggressive creatures. By utilizing cards like Mishra's Workshop and Ancient Tomb to quickly ramp up mana, the deck can flood the board with threatening threats like Glaring Fleshraker and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain. The inclusion of cards like Defense Grid and Pithing Needle provide disruption, while cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Mystic Forge offer additional utility and card advantage. The deck's ultimate goal is to quickly establish board dominance and pressure opponents into submission.

The main idea of this deck is to quickly assemble a board presence of powerful artifacts to overwhelm the opponent while disrupting their gameplay with cards like Wasteland and Null Rod. By combining efficient creatures and mana acceleration with disruptive elements like Thorn of Amethyst and Sphere of Resistance, the deck aims to take control of the game early and maintain its advantage through powerful artifacts like Golos, Tireless Pilgrim and Mystic Forge. The deck also has a toolbox of artifacts like Karn, the Great Creator and Crucible of Worlds to adapt to different situations and outmaneuver the opponent.

The deck focuses on utilizing powerful artifacts and creatures to quickly overwhelm the opponent. By using cards like Mishra's Workshop, Mana Crypt, and Ancient Tomb, the deck can ramp up quickly and get ahead on board presence. Tezzeret, Cruel Captain and Karn, the Great Creator provide additional threats and disruption, while Manifold Key and Time Vault offer a potential win condition. The deck aims to lock down the opponent's options with cards like Chalice of the Void and Pithing Needle, while maintaining card advantage with cards like Sensei's Divining Top and Mystic Forge.
